violations.text: Observed walk-in cooler in poor repair (not holding adequate ambient temperature) located in back storage area. PIC instructed to not store TCS foods in unit until service is provided. TCS foods were relocated to other refrigeration at time of inspection. PIC placed a service order for walk-in cooler at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following: -top of beverage coolers -chip bottom shelf -rice shelf -on side of snack zone shelf have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

Observed a hose connected to the faucet at the (mop sink). This faucet is lacking a backflow prevention device. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Observed several parts of floors located in retail area in poor repair. Per PIC service order has been placed. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

At the time of inspection, food items in the retail reach-in refrigeration were observed held at 42 to 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC, a workorder ticket is in place for the freezer units. PIC is to include refrigeration on workorder for any necessary adjustments. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
The walk-in freezer was observed to have an accumulation of ice build-up on the floor. Per the PIC, a workorder is in place for thawing of the walk-in freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
Old rodent droppings were observed on the floor near the mop sink. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023
